rules.description: If the computer does not start, then there is a general hardware issue.
If the computer does not start and there is no fan noise, then the problem is with the power supply (a more specific rule).
If the computer does not start and emits continuous beeping sounds, then the problem is with the motherboard or RAM.
If there is a motherboard or RAM issue and the beeps are short and continuous, then the RAM is faulty.
If the screen is black, then there is a display issue.
If there is a display issue and the fans are running, then the problem is with the graphics card (GPU).
If there is a display issue and the display cable is disconnected, then the problem is simply the cable.
If the system is slow, then there is a performance issue.
If there is a performance issue and the hard drive is making clicking noises, then the hard drive is failing.
If there is a performance issue and the temperature is very high, then the problem is with the cooling system.
If a blue screen error appears, then there is a system conflict.
If there is a system conflict and new drivers were recently installed, then the problem is with hardware drivers.
